title: centaurea stohlii
intro: Centaurea stohlii, also known as blue knapweed or stohli's knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Greece and Turkey, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Centaurea stohlii include its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve respiratory problems, and reduce fever, as well as its potential to act as a natural antacid and anti-diarrheal agent. Centaurea stohlii is traditionally prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in the form of dried flowers, leaves, or roots in various herbal preparations.
